assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files checked. Task 1: has Repository (trustify-backend), Target Branch (main), Description, Files to Create + Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 2: has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 3: has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create + Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 4: has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 5: has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create + Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 6: has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 6 task files: task-1-advisory-summary-model.md, task-2-advisory-summary-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-cache-invalidation.md, task-5-integration-tests.md, task-6-server-route-mounting.md. 6 is within the range [3, 10].
All 6 tasks have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' — Task 1 line 3-4, Task 2 line 3-4, Task 3 line 3-4, Task 4 line 3-4, Task 5 line 3-4, Task 6 line 3-4.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Task 3 and Task 4. Task 6 depends on Task 3. This is a valid DAG: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> {4, 6}, {3, 4} -> 5. No cycles exist.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ directory), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in repo manifest). Task 2 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists in manifest). Task 3 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing endpoints/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (exists). Task 4 modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (exists). Task 5 creates tests/api/sbom_advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing tests/api/ dir), modifies tests/Cargo.toml (exists). Task 6 modifies server/src/main.rs (exists). All paths are plausible within the repo structure.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map in impact-map.md lists specific changes: 'New model struct — AdvisorySeveritySummary response struct in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/', 'New service method — get_advisory_summary on SbomService in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs', 'New endpoint — GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary handler in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/', 'Cache invalidation — Modify advisory ingestion in modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs', 'Integration tests — New test file tests/api/sbom_advisory_summary.rs'. Each change specifies the concrete artifact, its purpose, and its file path.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' with exactly one repository name. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checklist items), '## Test Requirements' (with checklist items). Verified in all files: task-1 through task-6.

assertion_results.evidence: Modified files all exist in repo-backend.md manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (line 41), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (line 46), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (line 48), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (line 83), server/src/main.rs (line 107), tests/Cargo.toml (line 114). assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have '## Target Branch\nmain': task-1 line 7, task-2 line 7, task-3 line 7, task-4 line 7, task-5 line 7, task-6 line 7.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain digest lines at the end: Task 1: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:aa18a7007285856daa9215055c2bf861dc0661defdd57ecedcd7d4e6391313d2' (64 hex chars). Task 2: 'sha256-md:0dd83aa8d59effb7721bde3257c7efdda9b5370833f94ee121eeea24d9a282e1' (64 hex). Task 3: 'sha256-md:654230c60bdc9679e83c70ba0f7b4be69bbff8a7558af3ea347c3fecf7a7f757' (64 hex). Task 4: 'sha256-md:62899f1f5bfb5320769d054c70494ee2d7232f01a509cdeaf4f17e77a8e2f112' (64 hex). Task 5: 'sha256-md:ce43a43b50229616d3e7fbd9c9c7fbd478c206a414140ce29ecf2591e2be40d1' (64 hex). Task 6: 'sha256-md:879f010f78c5b932630947da5ec77db21d9586dde606e51d0033e52710bc7ebc' (64 hex). All are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:', with the correct marker format.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All tasks include convention references with the prescribed rationale format. Task 1: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Module pattern: follow the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ structure... Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs matching the convention's Rust module file scope.' Task 2: two convention references both with 'Applies: task modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' Task 3: three convention references all with 'Applies: task modifies/creates ... matching the convention's .rs file scope.' Task 4: 'Applies: task modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task creates tests/api/sbom_advisory_summary.rs matching the convention's test file scope.' Task 6: 'Applies: task modifies server/src/main.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed. All rationales follow the prescribed 'Applies: task modifies/creates <file> matching the convention's <scope>' format.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9001 has Priority: Major (not 'Undefined'). All 6 tasks include Jira Metadata with 'priority': {'name': 'Major'} in additional_fields. Task 1 line 52: '"priority": {"name": "Major"}'. Task 2 line 64. Task 3 line 73. Task 4 line 50. Task 5 line 68. Task 6 line 47. All tasks correctly inherit the priority.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9001 has Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (non-empty). CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both', meaning tasks should inherit fixVersions. All 6 tasks include 'fixVersions': [{'name': 'RHTPA 1.5.0'}] in additional_fields. Task 1 line 52, Task 2 line 64, Task 3 line 73, Task 4 line 50, Task 5 line 68, Task 6 line 47.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md line 38: 'Inherited fields propagated to tasks: priority=Major, fixVersion=RHTPA 1.5.0'. This explicitly states both the inherited priority (Major) and fixVersion (RHTPA 1.5.0) that were propagated to tasks.
